avalancheActivity.comment: In particular, even a small additional load can trigger a small avalanche on snowdrift accumulations. A triggered avalanche or an otherwise large Additional load may exceed the load-bearing capacity of the deep hard layers, triggering a deep and widespread (large) avalanche. Such weak layers are particularly abundant in the axis above the forest, but are not excluded elsewhere. Gliding avalanches also occur on steep smooth slopes.
avalancheActivity.highlights: New fallen snow in the high alpine regions, weak layers persist.

bulletins.highlights: The current snowpack pattern is uncommon and requires special attention and caution!

snowpackStructure.comment:
In the last 24 hours, several centimetres of New fallen snow have fallen above an altitude of around 1400 metres. Above 1700 m, snow fell on the hard surface of the old snowpack, while lower down it fell on breakable crust or soft ground.
The old snow cover is made up of layers of bonded snow that are already somewhat compacted. The cohesion of the layers of blown snow in snowdrift accumulations built up by southerly winds during the snowfall is weaker. In the old snow cover, there are several weak layers of shreds and sheared grains deep below the surface.

tendency.highlights: On Monday night it will become cloudy again, with a westerly breeze. Showers are possible during the day. The danger of avalanches will remain similar.

weatherForecast.comment: It will snow on Saturday and Sunday night. The snowfall level will initially be around 1400 m, but will drop below 1000 m by morning. Up to 15 cm of snow will fall. Northerly winds will blow in the 2nd half of the night, and will be strongest on Sunday morning. The wind will die down by the afternoon. It will clear from the north-west in the morning and be sunny during the day, mainly in the high mountains. It will be cooler, with a Zero-degree level around 1000 m during the day on Sunday, around -3 at 1500 m, around -4 at 2000 m in the morning and -7 °C in the afternoon.

avalancheActivity.comment: In particular, even a small additional load can trigger a small avalanche on snowdrift accumulations. Gliding avalanches also occur on steep, smooth slopes.

bulletins.highlights: Increased amount of new snow in the highest parts of the mid-mountains, snowdrift accumulations

snowpackStructure.comment: The old snow cover is made up of layers of bonded snow that are already somewhat compacted and mostly well connected. A depth hoar has formed at the interface between the snowpack and the ground.

tendency.highlights: On Monday night it will become cloudy again, with a westerly breeze. Showers are possible during the day. The danger of avalanches will remain similar.

Northerly winds will blow in the 2nd half of the night, and will be strongest on Sunday morning. The wind will die down by the afternoon. It will clear from the north-west in the morning and be sunny during the day, mainly in the high mountains. It will be cooler, with a Zero-degree level around 1000 m during the day on Sunday and around -3 at 1500 m.
